title: My Sleep Toolkit Card
description: Your personal quick-reference card with the sleep strategies that work best for you. Fill it in and download it to keep by your bedside.
instructions: Think back through everything you've learned in this course. Write down the specific techniques and routines that resonate most with you. This becomes your personal sleep toolkit — a quick reference for nights when sleep feels difficult.

items.label: My wind-down routine starts with:
items.placeholder: e.g., Dim lights at 9pm, herbal tea, 10 min reading

items.label: My go-to relaxation technique for falling asleep:
items.placeholder: e.g., 4-7-8 breathing, body scan, progressive muscle relaxation

items.label: When racing thoughts keep me up, I will:
items.placeholder: e.g., Worry dump journal, cognitive shuffle, constructive worry time earlier in the day

items.label: If I wake in the middle of the night:
items.placeholder: e.g., Stay in bed 20 min with body scan, then get up and read in dim light

items.label: My ideal sleep environment setup:
items.placeholder: e.g., 65-68°F, blackout curtains, white noise machine, phone in another room

items.label: My target sleep schedule:
items.placeholder: e.g., Bed by 10:30pm, wake at 6:30am, even on weekends

disclaimer: This card is a personal sleep wellness tool. If you experience persistent sleep difficulties despite consistent good habits, consult a healthcare provider or sleep specialist.
